description: Department of Labor news-card combining a photograph with an overlaid headline and an excerpted news quote. The top photo shows a group of students and an instructor on a workshop or factory floor: a woman in a green top (left) talking with several young workers wearing hard hats (white, orange numbered '51', a green sticker-covered helmet) and safety glasses, smiling. Across the lower-middle, large white serif typography reads 'Pell Grants for' (italic) above bold all-caps 'TRADE SCHOOLS!' Beneath, on a dark navy background, a white text box carries the 'EdNC' (EducationNC) logo and an excerpt: 'New federal legislation will allow students in short-term workforce training programs at North Carolina's 58 community colleges or other eligible institutions to use Pell Grants for tuition, fees, and other expenses, according to a press release from the N.C. Community College System. Before the legislation, Pell Grants could only cover the costs for curriculum-based programs lasting 15 weeks or more.'
notable_text: Pell Grants for TRADE SCHOOLS! / EdNC / New federal legislation will allow students in short-term workforce training programs at North Carolina's 58 community colleges... to use Pell Grants for tuition, fees, and other expenses...
